api_findings.script_bug: run_airllm.py passes 'cache_dir' kwarg which AirLLM does not accept; correct param is 'layer_shards_saving_path'
api_findings.gpu_default: AirLLM defaults device='cuda:0' — no GPU available on this machine

blocker_1_model_format.description: AirLLM asserts model.safetensors.index.json must exist. This file is only present in large models that HuggingFace stores as multiple shards.
blocker_1_model_format.small_model_format: Qwen2.5-0.5B uses a single model.safetensors (no index file) — incompatible with AirLLM's sharding logic.
blocker_1_model_format.large_model_format: OPT-6.7B would have model.safetensors.index.json, but requires full 13.5 GB download (only 4 GB cached — blocked).

blocker_2_no_gpu.description: AirLLM default device is cuda:0. CPU support is not officially documented. Even if CPU were used, float16 is not supported on CPU without special handling.
blocker_2_no_gpu.hardware: No CUDA GPU — Intel i5-1135G7 integrated graphics only

conclusion: AirLLM cannot run on this machine within time/resource constraints. Two hard blockers: (1) tiny models lack the sharded format AirLLM requires; (2) large models (OPT-6.7B) require download of ~13.5 GB which previously stalled. Additionally no GPU. Marking AirLLM experiment as BLOCKED.
next_step: Proceed to quantization experiment (llama.cpp / GGUF with Qwen2.5-7B Q4_K_M).
